creator: pavelivolkov@gmail.com
text:
Hello.
Sorry. Sqlite versions 3.7.15(.[12])+ don't pass 1000 atof1- tests on
i386 platform FreeBSD.
I'm reported of this in mail list: "General Discussion of SQLite
Database <sqlite-users@sqlite.org>".
The solution has yet been found. Did anyone help to find it?
Details can be found here:

log compilation for sqlite version 3.7.15.1:
https://docs.google.com/open?id=0Bz0ZXsXvwY_gRVFHejZjVlRmREU

log testing for sqlite version 3.7.15.1:
https://docs.google.com/open?id=0Bz0ZXsXvwY_gaTh4N1hFVTdiRWM

Unofficial port for the development and testing sqlite:
https://docs.google.com/open?id=0Bz0ZXsXvwY_gV3Nhd1ZrNlpTcE0

My operating system:
FreeBSD test 9.1-PRERELEASE FreeBSD 9.1-PRERELEASE #3: Sun Dec 30
16:52:52 MSK 2012     root@test:/usr/obj/usr/src/
sys/GENERIC  i386

and compiler:
cc (GCC) 4.2.1 20070831 patched [FreeBSD]

output of the following command: cpp -dM /dev/null | sort

On Mon, Jan 28, 2013 at 11:20 PM, Edwin Groothuis <edwin@freebsd.org> wrote:
> Maintainer of databases/sqlite3,
>
> Please note that PR ports/175656 has just been submitted.
>
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
>
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>     http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/175656
>
> --
> Edwin Groothuis via the GNATS Auto Assign Tool
> edwin@FreeBSD.org
